narration: Define a class named A with a public void method called show.
Inside the show method, create a for loop that iterates from 1 to 10.
Inside the loop, print the string 'Hi' to the console.
Create a second class named B with a similar show method.
Inside class B's show method, create a for loop that iterates from 1 to 10.
Inside the loop, print the string 'Hello' to the console.
In the main method, create an object of class A named obj1.
In the main method, create an object of class B named obj2.
Call the show method on obj1 followed by calling the show method on obj2.
Compile and run the code to observe that the methods execute sequentially.
Explain that the JVM executes the main method sequentially, waiting for one method call to finish before starting the next.
Modify class A to extend the Thread class.
Modify class B to extend the Thread class.
Replace the direct method calls with the start method for both obj1 and obj2.
Explain that calling start creates a new thread and internally invokes the run method.
Run the code and observe that the output still appears sequential due to the speed of execution.
Increase the loop iteration count from 10 to 100 in both classes.
Run the code again to observe the interleaved output of 'Hi' and 'Hello'.
Explain that the OS scheduler manages thread execution by time-sharing CPU cores.
